pdf_evidence: Green-boxed note on PDF p32: "Note that QNAM references the "parent" variable name with the addition of "DSC". Likewise, the label is a modification of the parent variable label."

PDF p32 body text: "For example, consider a CRF that collects the indication for an analgesic concomitant medication (CMINDC) using a list of prespecified values and an "Other, specify" field :" (trailing space before colon preserved as in PDF)
PDF p33 table under §4.2.7.2, first table header row: SCTEST | SCORRES | SCSTRESC

PDF p34 decision tree figure titled "Figure. Decision Tree for Populating --OBJ" under §4.2.7.4; verbatim is a free description per spec (not literal-checked); atom_type FIGURE correct; parent_section §4.2.7.4 confirmed; figure_ref pdf_p0034+middle consistent with figure placement.
PDF p35 dataset filename label "ae.xpt" appearing as a code/dataset reference under §4.2.8.3 Multiple Values for a Non-result Qualifier Variable; verbatim exactly matches.
PDF p36 §4.2.9 Variable Lengths, sub-bullet: "The length for variables that use controlled terminology can be set to the length of the longest term." Verbatim matches exactly.
PDF p37 §4.3.2 Controlled Terminology Text Case final sentence: "See Section 4.2.4, Text Case in Submitted Data." Verbatim matches exactly; cross_refs [§4.2.4] confirmed.
PDF p37 §4.3.6 Storing Topic Variables for General Domain Models: "For an Interventions domain, the topic variable is --TRT." Verbatim matches exactly.

findings: heading_level claimed as 1 but should be 2. Section 4.4 is a second-level heading (Chapter 4 = level 1, 4.x = level 2). sibling_index=4 is correct (4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4 = 4th sibling). All other fields correct.
pdf_evidence: PDF p38 heading: "4.4 Actual and Relative Time Assumptions" — clearly a 4.x numbered section, subordinate to Chapter 4. Document structure places 4.4 at level 2.
PDF p40 §4.4.2 second table row 3: "December 15, 2003 13:??:17 | Unknown minutes with known date, hours, and seconds | 2003-12-15T13:-:17" with row number 3 prefix. All cells match verbatim claim.
